title: ARC Scaling Challenge: An Open Falsification Toolkit for Recursive-Amplification Scaling Laws
description: An open toolkit and protocol set for independently testing — and attempting to falsify — claims about sequential versus parallel recursion scaling (the ARC Principle). Provides alpha/beta estimators, AIC/BIC model selection, falsification criteria, reproducible protocols, and synthetic example data. The robust, replicated finding is that the FORM of recursion governs the scaling exponent (parallel alpha ≈ 0; sequential super-linear within architecture); an early single-model estimate (alpha ≈ 2.24, DeepSeek R1) did not replicate cross-architecturally (cross-architecture fit ≈ 0.49). The sequential-beats-parallel result is independently corroborated by Sharma & Chopra (arXiv:2511.02309, 2025) and no priority is claimed for it; the bare d/(d+1) exponent is acknowledged prior art. Researchers are invited to replicate or falsify.
notes: Companion repository: arc-principle-validation (papers, results, harness). Canonical research hub: https://www.michaeldariuseastwood.com/research/
